  • Hide All Code

VC data, first sim results

Arnie Seong

02 March 2026

testing the VC waters

  • \(Z\) dimension \(R\) = 20, results only depend on first 5 \(Z\) variables,

  • \(X\) dimension \(p\) = 3,

Just to see if we can adequately capture the data generating functions, tested with \(n = 10k\), noise variance \(\sigma^2_\epsilon = 0\)

vanilla model

Just proceeding as usual without explicitly modeling the VC structure:

  • 2 hidden layers, 8 and 16 units;

  • \(\tau_0\) agnostic for all.

load(resfile_vanilla[1])
nn_mod <- torch_load(modfile_vanilla[1])
plot_b0_true()

plot_b1_true()

sim_res$sim_params$p_0
## [1] 11.5
dim_vec <- c(
  sim_res$sim_params$d_0,
  sim_res$sim_params$d_1,
  sim_res$sim_params$d_2,
  sim_res$sim_params$d_L
)
param_counts_from_dims(dim_vec)
## layer_1 layer_2 layer_3   TOTAL 
##     480     324     102     906
varnames <- c(
  paste0("z", 1:sim_res$sim_params$R),
  paste0("x", 1:sim_res$sim_params$p)
)
showvars <- c(
  paste0("z", 1:5),
  paste0("x", 1:sim_res$sim_params$p)
)
true_vec <- varnames %in% showvars

results

sim 1

loss_pltfcn(sim_res, burn = 5e4)$mse_plt

loss_pltfcn(sim_res, burn = 5e4)$kl_plt

kmat_tc <- sim_res$kappa_tc_mat
kmat_gl <- sim_res$kappa_mat
kmat_lo <- sim_res$kappa_local_mat
kmat_fc <- sim_res$kappa_fc_mat

colnames(sim_res$alpha_mat)  <- 
  colnames(kmat_tc) <- 
  colnames(kmat_gl) <-
  colnames(kmat_lo) <- varnames

if (!is.null(sim_res$kappa_fc_mat)){
  kmat_fc <- sim_res$kappa_fc_mat
  colnames(kmat_fc) <- varnames
}

varmat_pltfcn(
  sim_res$alpha_mat,
  y_name = "alpha",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

varmat_pltfcn(
  kmat_gl,
  y_name = "GLOBAL kappa",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

varmat_pltfcn(
  kmat_lo,
  y_name = "LOCAL kappa",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

varmat_pltfcn(
  kmat_tc,
  y_name = "tau-corrected kappas",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

if (!is.null(sim_res$kappa_fc_mat)){
  varmat_pltfcn(
    kmat_fc,
    y_name = "frob-corrected kappas",
    burn = 0,
    show_vars = showvars
  )$all_vars_plt
}

err_by_max_bfdr(
  kmat_lo[nrow(kmat_lo), ],
  true_vec = true_vec
)$plt_fdrs + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "using local kappas as dropout probabilities"
  )

err_by_max_bfdr(
  kmat_gl[nrow(kmat_gl), ],
  true_vec = true_vec
)$plt_fdrs + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "using global kappas as dropout probabilities"
  )

err_by_max_bfdr(
  kmat_tc[nrow(kmat_tc),],
  true_vec = true_vec
)$plt_fdrs + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "tau-corrected kappas as dropout probabilities"
  )

if (!is.null(sim_res$kappa_fc_mat)){
  err_by_max_bfdr(
    kmat_fc[nrow(kmat_fc),],
    true_vec = true_vec
  )$plt_fdrs + 
    labs(
      subtitle = "frob-corrected kappas as dropout probabilities"
    )
}

sim 2

load(resfile_vanilla[2])
nn_mod <- torch_load(modfile_vanilla[2])
loss_pltfcn(sim_res, burn = 5e4)$mse_plt

loss_pltfcn(sim_res, burn = 5e4)$kl_plt

kmat_tc <- sim_res$kappa_tc_mat
kmat_gl <- sim_res$kappa_mat
kmat_lo <- sim_res$kappa_local_mat
kmat_fc <- sim_res$kappa_fc_mat

colnames(sim_res$alpha_mat)  <- 
  colnames(kmat_tc) <- 
  colnames(kmat_gl) <-
  colnames(kmat_lo) <- varnames

if (!is.null(sim_res$kappa_fc_mat)){
  kmat_fc <- sim_res$kappa_fc_mat
  colnames(kmat_fc) <- varnames
}

varmat_pltfcn(
  sim_res$alpha_mat,
  y_name = "alpha",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

varmat_pltfcn(
  kmat_gl,
  y_name = "GLOBAL kappa",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

varmat_pltfcn(
  kmat_lo,
  y_name = "LOCAL kappa",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

varmat_pltfcn(
  kmat_tc,
  y_name = "tau-corrected kappas",
  burn = 0,
  show_vars = showvars
)$all_vars_plt

if (!is.null(sim_res$kappa_fc_mat)){
  varmat_pltfcn(
    kmat_fc,
    y_name = "frob-corrected kappas",
    burn = 0,
    show_vars = showvars
  )$all_vars_plt
}

err_by_max_bfdr(
  kmat_lo[nrow(kmat_lo), ],
  true_vec = true_vec
)$plt_fdrs + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "using local kappas as dropout probabilities"
  )

err_by_max_bfdr(
  kmat_gl[nrow(kmat_gl), ],
  true_vec = true_vec
)$plt_fdrs + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "using global kappas as dropout probabilities"
  )

err_by_max_bfdr(
  kmat_tc[nrow(kmat_tc),],
  true_vec = true_vec
)$plt_fdrs + 
  labs(
    subtitle = "tau-corrected kappas as dropout probabilities"
  )

if (!is.null(sim_res$kappa_fc_mat)){
  err_by_max_bfdr(
    kmat_fc[nrow(kmat_fc),],
    true_vec = true_vec
  )$plt_fdrs + 
    labs(
      subtitle = "frob-corrected kappas as dropout probabilities"
    )
}

VC mod testing

Explicitly modeling the VC structure:

  • input \(X, Z\) vars separately. First \(L-1\) layers take only \(Z\) variables as input.

  • First \(L-1\) layers (all but last) used to estimate \(\beta_j(Z)\)’s and perform variable selection on the \(Z\) variables;

  • layer \(L\) (output layer) does not have a weight matrix; only exists to estimate final scale parameters for the \(\beta_j\)’s / provide variable selection on the \(X\) variables:

    • layer \(L\) computes \(\hat{y_i} = \beta_0(Z_i) \times \lambda_0 \tau_L + \sum_{j=1}^p \beta_j(Z_i) \times \lambda_j \tau_L \times x_{i,j}\)

first VC sim

  • data: R = 20, p = 3, n = 10k, noise = 0

  • model: 3 regular hshoe layers + vc layer

  • prior tau_R (used in first layer): sim_res$sim_params$prior_tau_R

  • prior tau_p (used in last layer): sim_res$sim_params$prior_tau_p

  • agnostic tau otherwise

  • 2 hidden layers, 8 and 16 units;

  • \(\tau_0\) agnostic for all.

dim_list <- sim_res$sim_params[grepl("d_", names(sim_res$sim_params))]
dim_vec <- do.call(c, dim_list)
print(dim_vec)
##  d_0  d_1  d_2 d_p1  d_L 
##   20    8   16    4    1
# param_counts_from_dims is slight overcount (written for regular hshoe layers)
# subtract (d_in*d_out + 1)*2 from param counts b/c vc layer has no weights/bias
param_counts_from_dims(dim_vec) 
## layer_1 layer_2 layer_3 layer_4   TOTAL 
##     420     324     204      30     978
varnames <- c(
  paste0("z", 1:sim_res$sim_params$R),
  "b_int",
  paste0("b", 1:sim_res$sim_params$p)
)
showvars <- c(
  paste0("z", 1:5),
  "b_int",
  paste0("b", 1:sim_res$sim_params$p)
)
true_vec <- varnames %in% showvars

results

load(resfile_vc0[1])
nn_mod <- torch_load(modfile_vc0[1])

sim 1

sim 2

comments

Looks like it was nowhere near convergence. Actually kind of heartening that maybe it can do better, but how long will we need to train? maybe need to up the learning rate a bunch.

all agnostic 3L VC sim

  • data: R = 20, p = 3, n = 10k, noise = 0

  • model: 3 regular hshoe layers + vc layer

  • (agnostic) prior tau_R (used in first layer): sim_res$sim_params$prior_tau_R

  • (agnostic) prior tau_p (used in last layer): sim_res$sim_params$prior_tau_p

  • 2 hidden layers, 8 and 16 units;

  • \(\tau_0\) agnostic for all.

nn_mod$children
## $fc1
## An `nn_module` containing 420 parameters.
## 
## -- Parameters ------------------------------------------------------------------
## * sa_mu: Float [1:1]
## * sa_logvar: Float [1:1]
## * sb_mu: Float [1:1]
## * sb_logvar: Float [1:1]
## * atilde_mu: Float [1:20]
## * atilde_logvar: Float [1:20]
## * btilde_mu: Float [1:20]
## * btilde_logvar: Float [1:20]
## * weight_mu: Float [1:8, 1:20]
## * weight_logvar: Float [1:8, 1:20]
## * bias_mu: Float [1:8]
## * bias_logvar: Float [1:8]
## 
## $fc2
## An `nn_module` containing 324 parameters.
## 
## -- Parameters ------------------------------------------------------------------
## * sa_mu: Float [1:1]
## * sa_logvar: Float [1:1]
## * sb_mu: Float [1:1]
## * sb_logvar: Float [1:1]

## * atilde_logvar: Float [1:8]
## * btilde_mu: Float [1:8]
## * btilde_logvar: Float [1:8]

## * weight_logvar: Float [1:16, 1:8]
## * bias_mu: Float [1:16]
## * bias_logvar: Float [1:16]
## 
## $fc3
## An `nn_module` containing 204 parameters.
## 
## -- Parameters ------------------------------------------------------------------
## * sa_mu: Float [1:1]
## * sa_logvar: Float [1:1]
## * sb_mu: Float [1:1]
